---
title: "2.1 Asking useful questions"
---

## Explanation

Useful questions are specific. Instead of asking whether something is correct,
ask about inputs, outputs, assumptions, edge cases, complexity, and validation.

Weak:

```text
Is this function correct?
```

Better:

```text
For this function, list the expected input type, output type,
edge cases, and tests that would catch a wrong implementation.
```

If you do not yet know what to ask, start honestly. Ask the AI agent to suggest
what should be checked, then use that list to make more specific questions.

```text
I do not yet know what to check in this function.
Suggest a review checklist: inputs, outputs, assumptions,
edge cases, numerical accuracy, performance, and validation tests.
```

## Things to look up

- How to ask coding questions
- Input and output
- Edge case
- Failure mode
- Prompting for code review
- Review checklist
- Brainstorming

## Exercise

You are given an unknown function that computes a numerical quantity from an
array. First, write one prompt asking an AI agent to suggest what should be
checked. Then write five specific follow-up questions you would ask to
understand the function.

## Notes for the exercise

- It is acceptable to start by asking for possible review criteria.
- Turn the proposed criteria into specific questions before asking for judgment.
- Include at least one question about input.
- Include at least one question about output.
- Include at least one question about boundary cases.
- Include at least one question about validation.
- Avoid the phrase "Is it correct?" unless you also specify a criterion.
